purpose: Span endpoint bulk T extraction from existing XY cut files + Ri characteristic length audit
scope: Salt 2/3/4 Jin mainline cases; 6 spans × 3 cases; t=7915s/7618s/10000s

key_findings.ri_characteristic_length: D_h = 22.098 mm (correct for pipe mixed convection). 5.7% test section bias, ~2% impact on upcomer median Ri.

open_questions_for_user: Cooler cut planes only capture 45-51% of Q_cooler — full cooler energy balance requires cut planes at reducer boundaries. Accept as limitation or add new sample points?
Upcomer endpoint T values are dominated by recirculation. For 1D model validation, is the heater dT cross-check sufficient, or do we need upcomer segment T profile separately?
Right_leg (downcomer) T values are confounded by recirculation and bend geometry. Should we add a mid-leg cut plane away from bends for cleaner downcomer T sampling?
